reason: Both answers correctly identify default-alive as giving leverage, land on ~17% dilution, and correctly recommend taking the deal conditional on a concrete use-of-funds acceleration plan — hitting the core decision-relevant points. B is slightly stronger because it explicitly flags terms beyond valuation (board seat, pro-rata) which A omits (A only mentions renegotiating price), and B's runway framing (14mo thin buffer -> 3+ years of safety, risk asymmetry of regret) is a clean, correct, decision-useful addition. A's unique contribution (founder-ambition test: venture path vs. lifestyle business) is a nice touch but doesn't outweigh B's more complete coverage of deal terms. Both are similarly concise and neither pads unnecessarily.
